title: Today's Tech Brief: Proposed new US funding rules: We can cancel any grant at any time
summary: US funding rules, AI sticker shock, and the impact on tech

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to today's tech brief. I've got a couple of interesting stories to share with you. The US government is proposing new funding rules that could significantly impact the way grants are handled. According to a report from Ars Technica, the Office of Management and Budget is trying again to cripple US science by introducing a rule that would allow the government to cancel any grant at any time. This move is raising eyebrows in the scientific community, as it could lead to a lot of uncertainty and potentially stifle innovation.

Meanwhile, corporate America is experiencing a bit of AI sticker shock. A report from Axios notes that companies are realizing just how expensive it is to implement and maintain AI systems. I guess you could say they're getting a taste of their own medicine - or rather, their own AI-powered bill. It's a reminder that while AI can be incredibly powerful, it's not a magic solution that's going to come cheap.

The proposed funding rules are an interesting example of how policy can impact the tech industry. If implemented, they could have a chilling effect on scientific research and development. My read is that this move is likely to be met with resistance from the scientific community and tech industry leaders. It's a complex issue, but it's definitely one to keep an eye on.

My quick read: today's tech stories are less about one giant thunderclap and more about a lively weather pattern. A little platform drama here, a little artificial intelligence irony there, and suddenly the industry has enough material for a very caffeinated morning show.

sources.title: Proposed new US funding rules: We can cancel any grant at any time
AI sticker shock hits corporate America - Axios
